creator: parag@codegen.com
text:
The port lang/slib fails to install.  It builds fine, but fails
    with this error:

	# make install
	===>  Installing for slib-2c9
	===>   Generating temporary packing list
	#       moved here from author's makefile
	/usr/bin/touch -r /usr/ports/lang/slib/work/slib/slibcat /usr/local/lib/scheme48/slibcat
	touch: /usr/local/lib/scheme48/slibcat: No such file or directory
	*** Error code 1

	Stop in /usr/ports/lang/slib.
	*** Error code 1

	Stop in /usr/ports/lang/slib.
	*** Error code 1

	Stop in /usr/ports/lang/slib.
    
    
    Creating the directory scheme48 allows the install to finish.

	
	# mkdir /usr/local/lib/scheme48
	# make install                  
	===>  Installing for slib-2c9
	===>   Generating temporary packing list
	#       moved here from author's makefile
	/usr/bin/touch -r /usr/ports/lang/slib/work/slib/slibcat /usr/local/lib/scheme48/slibcat
	/bin/mkdir -p /usr/local/share/slib
	install -c -o root -g wheel -m 444 /usr/ports/lang/slib/work/slib/*.scm /usr/local/share/slib
	===>   Registering installation for slib-2c9
	#

Fix: 

The port's Makefile performs the touch.  It should make sure the
	target directory exists or create it before trying to touch the file.
How-To-Repeat: 
	cd /usr/ports/lang/slib
	make install
